turns.user_message: I used to get so excited about weekend plans, but lately I just stare at the calendar and feel completely blank. I tried picking up a hobby I really loved a few months ago, but it just felt like a chore I had to finish. I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch that will pass on its own soon. I just wish the spark would come back without me having to force it so much.

It has been weeks now and the same dull routine keeps repeating itself every single day. I remember when simple things like a good meal or a nice walk actually brought me some kind of joy. Now I just go through the checklist of daily tasks and wait for the clock to hit bedtime. I am starting to realize this isn't just a passing mood anymore, and it is really wearing me down. I keep hoping tomorrow will feel different, but nothing ever really changes.

I honestly cannot remember what it feels like to actually care about anything happening around me anymore. I used to get so wrapped up in the little details of life, but now everything just washes over me like gray water. I try to force myself to react to things that should matter, but my mind stays completely flat and empty. It is exhausting pretending to be engaged when there is absolutely nothing left inside to fuel it. I just exist in this heavy quiet and watch the days blur together without a single ripple.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be.
